title: Safely Fixing Nose Asymmetry and a Dorsal Hump: Expert Injector Techniques
filename: Safely Fixing Nose Asymmetry and a Dorsal Hump： Expert Injector Techniques.en.vtt
excerpt: In this case you're going to see a patient who did not like her nose she wasn't even clear on all the reasons why she has a dorsal bump and you can see by the shape of it that is more dominant on her left side this means what she sees in certain lights is a different shaped nose …

segments.text: in this case you're going to see a patient who did not like her nose she wasn't even clear on all the reasons why she has a dorsal bump and you can see by the shape of it that is more dominant on her left side this means what she sees in certain lights is a different shaped nose it also goes alongside a flattening of the tip of her nose which meant she had a very slight curvature alongside the complexity so in this case you will see me treat the nose in order to simplify straighten up the lines of the nose unfortunately there was an artery exactly where I wouldn't want it so I switched technique so you'll see me deal with how to inject when you know there's a blood vessel in the way and still get a great result it's particularly interesting to see the tip of her nose treated and the safety steps that I took in order to validate that there was room to do this procedure I hope you learn a lot from it so I'm scanning again cuz unlike the laella there actually was quite there were quite a few little vessels here so I'm just going to refresh myself to the depth of those vessels [Music]

I actually don't see any in the area I'm most interested in I'm there's a slight inward curve here which is part of this why from certain angles it will look less straight than from others very subtle but that's what I'm trying to see now as I inject to choose the most efficient place to hide this little bump here so I'm in position of aspirated negative compress the arteries that I'd worry about with the eyes and then we do less than what it would take to block a vessel anyway 05 I'm going to move it slightly repat the same process aspirate on periostium give it time compress the vessels little injection seeing the tissue rise which is all reassuring

again small volumes each time see this curvature this bump here that's what we're trying to hide done a good job but there's a little Gap here go with bevel down [Music] so that now looks from this aspect to be straight it's just where whether we can now blend in the lower tip using some of the same ideas really so I can see a dorsal nasal archery running here it is running right

where I might inject so it makes me want to maybe use a canula and go off the midline I don't usually like I don't like using canulas along the dorsum for exactly the reason but if I'm going at 90° to the archery then I feel much safer so because this vessel is running like that it's right where I might place a needle but if I go midline and go with the canula then I'm at 90° to that vessel the chances of in injecting significant filler is low no bleeding is good same principles as we always have with using canula which is it nice and mobile nothing wrong with [Music]

[Music] aspirating gener add a small amount of product check no blanching there's one downside with an entry point here is if you get swelling it's going to look like the nose is more curved than it really is [Music]

so aspirating small injection as it if it pops through or there's a lot of resistance I'll stop and aspirate again and then small volumes each time the straightness of the nose is interrupted by a very slight Flat Point here which I know noticed when you were talking as you pulled up it kind of straightened it so we could put a tiny bit here just to make that straighter yeah now before I do this I'm always checking how much room is there does this tissue move easily so the tip is a little bit firm I wouldn't feel confident adding much here but just above it the tissue raises quite easily

so I'll probably put my needle down to about that level and just try and get a little gentle curve here and it'll be a very small amount of product go through the same Co now the tip is lower risk for V for blocking of vessel because there are mainly just capillaries there but it's higher risk for blocking capillaries so compressing capillaries which is slightly different compression causes redness that persists whereas if you block an artery then obviously you're not getting any blood at all unless that's different much higher risk so I'm going to put a little gentle curve on my Canyon when I tilt the can I should see the effect that I want I don't want any fillow round where I'm injecting going to

injecting going to aspirate see the tip of the canula here and then gently inject it's five well worth it but made a really nice difference hopefully I can catch it on the side of the camera if you turn your head away from me hope you can see her nose is now completely straight we had a before then a flat point just here so very subtle yeah that Precision sculpting isn't it it's like a real Precision I can't wait to see it oh wow oh that's made oh my God with my nose oh my God I

love it oh w that's made a massive difference that's made a huge difference but really subtle but I can see a massive difference oh my God I love my nose I can't believe I'm looking at my nose and I actually love my nose God oh my God look at my nose I can't believe the difference that's made to my nose so good isn't it wow I've hated my nose for so many years look at my nose oh my God I can't believe oh yeah it's made a massive difference but really subtle but look at my nose oh my God I can't oh I'm so happy for you it's my nose that really cuz I've looked at my nose for so long you know how many times you look in the mirror and I hate it and how many times I've got it and gone you know just try to what if I just do that a little bit I'm sure that would look better I can't believe that I actually
can't believe that has been done wow [Music]
